title: Simulation Task
description: Design a social simulator to generate daily mobility trajectories of urban residents under an out-of-distribution (OOD) setting (N2A: Normal-to-Abnormal). A trajectory is an ordered sequence of (location, time) visits: {(l0,t0),(l1,t1),...,(ln,tn)}. In this task, the simulator MUST be trained/fitted only on normal-period records (2019-2020), while model selection / calibration MUST use abnormal-period targets (2021) as validation. Final performance MUST be reported on a held-out 2021 test split.

data_files.1921Y.json:
Resident daily trajectories across multiple years. For THIS TASK (N2A Online OOD), split by date into three disjoint subsets:
(A) TRAIN_NORMAL: keep ONLY records where date is within [2019-01-01, 2020-12-31]. Used for training/fitting priors/Pattern/Persona ONLY.
(B) V_CALIB_ABNORMAL: keep ONLY records where date is within [2021-01-01, 2021-12-31] AND assigned to the 2021-calib split. Used for calibration/model selection (best_params, early stop) ONLY.
(C) V_TEST_ABNORMAL: keep ONLY records where date is within [2021-01-01, 2021-12-31] AND assigned to the 2021-test split. Used ONLY once for final report.
data_files.poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.j: Maps each full POI name (e.g., "Rest Area#1061") to its latitude/longitude. Use it to get coordinates for each visited stop (for both 2019-2020 fitting and 2021 evaluation).
data_files.catto.json: Maps base POI name (suffix removed; e.g., "Rest Area") to a semantic category (e.g., "Travel & Transport"). Use it to compute category-based metrics.

overall_simulation_design.objective: Generate realistic daily mobility trajectories (ordered sequences of (POI, time) visits) for urban residents under an out-of-distribution Normal-to-Abnormal (N2A) setting: fit baseline behavior using only 2019–2020 records, tune/calibrate abnormal-shift parameters using a 2021 validation split, and report final performance on a held-out 2021 test split.

initialization.description: Load and parse 1921Y.json into per-user, per-day sequences; split by year into normal (2019–2020) vs abnormal (2021). Build POI catalog with lat/lon from poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.json and map fine categories to super-categories using catto.json. Initialize each Resident with baseline (normal-period) learned distributions (time-of-day, chain length, category/POI transitions, anchor POIs). Initialize global abnormal-shift parameters (to be calibrated) that perturb baseline distributions when simulating 2021.

fields: top-level values (daily activity strings containing dates, locations, times)
data_reference.derivation: Parse each string 'Activities at YYYY-MM-DD: ...' into date and ordered visits of (Category#POI_ID, HH:MM:SS); year determines normal (2019–2020) vs abnormal (2021). Join POI lat/lon by matching POI identifier strings to poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.json entries; map Category -> super-category via catto.json when available.

overall_simulation_design.execution: 1) Fit baseline per-user and global mobility models on 2019–2020 only (e.g., distributions over start time, stop count, inter-visit time gaps, super-category transitions, POI choice kernels conditioned on category and distance). 2) Define abnormal-shift mechanisms with free parameters (e.g., category-mix reweighting, increased/decreased mobility radius, increased infrastructure/transit propensity, time-shift of activities). 3) Calibrate abnormal-shift parameters by simulating 2021 validation days and optimizing an objective over evaluation metrics (trajectory similarity, distributional match) against 2021 ground truth. 4) Freeze baseline + calibrated shift and evaluate on 2021 held-out test split. 5) Online OOD context window rule: For each 2021 target day, construct a 7-day context window using only days strictly earlier than the target day; this window may include earlier 2021 days and may backfill from late 2020; never use target-day or future days.

fields: visit time tokens HH:MM:SS within daily strings
data_reference.derivation: Observed times are second-resolved; simulator can operate in minute granularity while outputting HH:MM:SS by rounding/stochastic seconds jitter (designed).
Each visit location is a POI identifier string (Category#id).

population_size.value: number of unique user IDs in 1921Y.json (one agent per key)

data_reference.derivation: Count keys in the JSON object; each key defines one resident agent.

scale_granularity.rationale: Trajectories are day-level sequences with intra-day timestamps; minute-level simulation supports feasibility constraints via travel-time approximations while matching the dataset’s timestamp structure. POI-level resolution is required because ground truth uses Category#POI_ID and POI lat/lon is available for distance-based plausibility.

roles.name: Resident

data_reference.derivation: Use JSON key as resident_id.

From 2019–2020 visits, compute top-K frequent POIs; additionally mark any POI whose category is 'Home' as an anchor when present (data-derived), otherwise rely on frequency-only heuristic (designed fallback).
For each 2019–2020 day, extract first visit time; fit a distribution per user (e.g., histogram/mixture).
For each 2019–2020 day, count visits; fit a per-user distribution.
Compute successive time gaps within 2019–2020 days; fit distribution (conditioned on previous/next category optionally).

Extract Category from each location token; optionally map to super-category via catto.json; fit Markov transition probabilities with smoothing at super-category level if category not found in catto.json.

Given chosen category, choose POI via a combination of (a) empirical POI popularity from 2019–2020 visits (data-derived from 1921Y.json) and (b) distance-decay from current POI using lat/lon (data-derived). Weighting between (a) and (b) is calibratable (mixed).

dynamic_states.name: current_day
dynamic_states.update_rule: Advance to next simulation date; for calibration/evaluation, simulate only dates in the selected 2021 validation/test split; for generation, dates are sampled from target set.

data_reference.derivation: Use available dates to define candidate simulation days (data-derived); selecting which days to simulate per split is designed by holdout plan.

dynamic_states.name: current_poi
dynamic_states.update_rule: Set to the most recently generated POI in the trajectory sequence.

dynamic_states.name: current_time
dynamic_states.update_rule: Set to generated time-of-day; increment by sampled inter-event gap subject to feasibility constraints.

data_reference.derivation: Time-of-day distributions fit from 2019–2020; feasibility adjustment (e.g., minimum travel time) is designed using POI distances.

dynamic_states.name: daily_plan
dynamic_states.update_rule: At day start, sample stop_count, start_time, and a sequence of activity categories; then instantiate POIs and times sequentially.

fields: per-day sequences of categories and times
data_reference.derivation: Plan templates and distributions learned from normal data; abnormal adjustments applied via exogenous shift parameters calibrated on 2021 validation.

dynamic_states.name: visited_sequence
dynamic_states.update_rule: Append each generated (POI, time) as simulation progresses.

roles.construction_from_data: Create one Resident agent per user ID in 1921Y.json. Parse 2019–2020 days to estimate baseline distributions and transition models. Build POI lookup table from poi_category_192021_longitude_latitude.json by indexing on the POI identifier string. Optionally map fine categories to super-categories using catto.json for smoothing and OOD robustness.
roles.update_from_data: During calibration/evaluation, for a given 2021 day the agent generates a trajectory using baseline models plus abnormal-shift parameters. No online learning from 2021 ground truth during simulation; only outer-loop calibration uses 2021 validation targets to adjust global shift parameters.

roles.name: CalibrationController

dynamic_states.name: current_parameter_vector
dynamic_states.update_rule: Update via black-box optimization (e.g., Bayesian optimization / evolutionary search / random search) to minimize validation loss computed against 2021 validation set.

dynamic_states.name: experiment_log
dynamic_states.update_rule: Append each trial’s params, seed, metrics, and artifacts.

roles.construction_from_data: Controller reads parsed datasets and defines train/validation/test partitions per holdout plan. It does not exist in the raw data; it is introduced to meet the requirement of calibrating using 2021 validation while fitting baseline on 2019–2020.
roles.update_from_data: Uses 2021 validation ground truth from 1921Y.json to compute objective; updates parameter vector based on optimization routine.

layers.name: calibration_loop
layers.edge_rule: Controller broadcasts a candidate abnormal-shift parameter vector and simulation configuration (seed, day subset); Residents simulate trajectories for the requested days; outputs are returned for scoring.

layers.name: shared_environment_lookup
layers.edge_rule: No direct resident-to-resident influence; all residents share the same POI catalog, category taxonomy, and global shift parameters.

interaction_topology.protocol: Per calibration iteration: (1) Controller selects parameter vector θ and simulation seed(s). (2) For each resident/day in the calibration subset, residents generate trajectories using θ. (3) Controller computes metrics vs 2021 ground truth and updates θ. After tuning, run evaluation on held-out 2021 test split and report metrics.

information_propagation.topology: none (no explicit social diffusion in provided data)
information_propagation.mechanism: Residents do not exchange messages; any global changes are modeled via exogenous abnormal-shift parameters applied uniformly or stratified by resident clusters (optional, designed).

data_reference.derivation: Data contains per-user sequences without any explicit social links or interactions; therefore diffusion is not directly supported.

exogenous_signals.name: abnormal_period_shift
exogenous_signals.effect_on_agents: Applies when simulating dates in 2021: reweights category/super-category choice, modifies stop-count distribution, shifts start times, adjusts travel-radius/distance-decay, and adjusts propensity to include infrastructure categories (e.g., Toll Booth/Tunnel/Rest Area/Platform) as intermediate stops.
exogenous_signals.bounds: Calibration bounds set to reasonable multiplicative/shift ranges around baseline (e.g., category weight multipliers in [0.25, 4], time shifts in [-120,+120] minutes, distance-decay scale multipliers in [0.5, 2.5]).

data_reference.derivation: Signal is designed as a parameterized mechanism; its parameters are calibrated to match distributional properties of 2021 trajectories.

exogenous_signals.name: day_type_proxy
exogenous_signals.effect_on_agents: Optional: adjust schedule templates for weekday/weekend using only date-derived heuristics; used to condition start time and stop-count distributions.
exogenous_signals.bounds: Binary {weekday, weekend} inferred from date; if timezone/calendar assumptions required, treat as heuristic with standard Gregorian calendar.

data_reference.derivation: Fit per-user and global models from normal period only.
Index POIs by poi_id and by category; compute distances as needed.
Lookup for each fine category; if missing, treat super-category as 'Unknown' (designed fallback).

by_role.policy_form: Hierarchical generative policy per day: (1) Sample start time and stop count from baseline distributions perturbed by abnormal shift (if 2021). (2) Sample a sequence of activity categories using a Markov model (category or super-category) with optional time-of-day conditioning and abnormal reweighting. (3) For each category, sample a concrete POI using a mixture of (a) personal historical preference (2019–2020 POI visit frequency) and (b) spatial distance-decay from current POI using lat/lon; optionally enforce feasibility by ensuring time gaps exceed a minimum travel-time proxy derived from distance. (4) Output ordered (POI, time) visits.

data_reference.derivation: Parse 2021 dates/visits for validation subset.

by_role.policy_form: Outer-loop calibration policy: propose θ, run simulation on 2021 validation subset, compute objective from metrics, update θ using selected black-box optimization strategy.

fields: date token in each daily record string: 'Activities at YYYY-MM-DD'
data_reference.derivation: Parse YYYY-MM-DD and sort chronologically; year used for regime split.

holdout_plan.train_range: Fit baseline only on dates with year in {2019, 2020}. Within 2021, do not use for baseline fitting.
holdout_plan.validation_range: Within 2021 dates, use first 80% of dates in chronological order as validation for abnormal-shift calibration (per user or globally; implement globally by date ordering). (FIXED SPLIT RULE: per-user-by-date only) For each resident independently, sort that resident's 2021 day-records by date ascending; assign the first 80% of that resident's 2021 records to V_calib and the last 20% to V_test (deterministic, no global mixing). If a resident has fewer than 5 total 2021 records, exclude that resident entirely from calibration/test with explicit logging (default), or assign all their 2021 records to V_test only (allowed fallback) — but never include them in V_calib.
holdout_plan.notes: Final test is the remaining last 20% of 2021 dates chronologically. If some users have sparse 2021 records, apply the 80/20 rule per-user to preserve personalization (designed choice); record which approach is used. Split strategy is fixed to per-user-by-date as specified above; do not use global-by-date splitting.

metrics.name: DARD
metrics.definition: Daily Activity Rhythm Divergence: a symmetric divergence between simulated vs ground-truth daily activity-rhythm distributions over time-of-day bins (optionally conditioned on activity categories), capturing phase shifts and missing/extra peaks in the daily schedule.

metrics.name: STVD
metrics.definition: Spatio-Temporal Visit Distribution distance: a transportation-based distance (e.g., Wasserstein/EMD) between simulated vs ground-truth empirical distributions of visits in joint (space, time) space, capturing coupled footprint errors such as correct locations at wrong times (and vice versa).

simulation_evaluation.comparison_method: Compute metrics on the 2021 validation split for each calibration trial; select theta minimizing a weighted sum of metrics (objective). After calibration, compute the same metrics on the 2021 test split and report. Hard constraint: best_params selection and early stopping must use ONLY V_calib; V_test must be evaluated exactly once after selection and must not affect any choice (no tuning, no re-selection, no peeking).

calibratable_parameters.name: theta_cat_weight_multiplier_by_supercategory
calibratable_parameters.notes: Reweights baseline category/super-category selection in 2021 to match abnormal mix.

calibratable_parameters.name: theta_stop_count_multiplier
calibratable_parameters.notes: Scales expected number of daily stops in 2021 vs baseline.

calibratable_parameters.name: theta_distance_decay_scale
calibratable_parameters.notes: Controls travel radius by scaling distance sensitivity in POI choice.

calibratable_parameters.name: theta_preference_vs_distance_mixture
calibratable_parameters.notes: Mixture weight: 1.0=personal preference only; 0.0=distance-only within category.

calibratable_parameters.name: theta_infrastructure_stop_bonus
calibratable_parameters.notes: Boosts probability of selecting travel/infrastructure-like categories as intermediate stops in 2021.
